verdict: AI displacement risk is moderate, with some tasks being automated but human judgment and critical thinking still essential.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, and 4 are routine cognitive tasks that can be automated by AI, such as reviewing prescriptions and analyzing prescribing trends.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: Task 3 requires human judgment and expertise in pharmacology, which is protected by regulatory moats such as licensure requirements and statutory liability.

reasoning_steps.title: Human Judgment and Critical Thinking
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks that require human judgment and critical thinking, such as advising customers on medication selection and dispensing medications safely, are less likely to be automated.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Summary of pharmacist role and responsibilities

adjacent_careers.name: Clinical Research Coordinator
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Both roles require human judgment and critical thinking, but clinical research coordinators may have more limited responsibilities.

adjacent_careers.name: Pharmacy Technician
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Pharmacy technicians perform tasks such as filling prescriptions and processing transactions, which are less likely to be automated.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered medication recommendation system approved by FDA
recent_developments.impact: This development may impact the role of pharmacists in recommending medications to patients.

recent_developments.headline: FDA clears more AI diagnostic tools each quarter
recent_developments.impact: Radiology, pathology, and dermatology are seeing AI second-readers move into routine clinical workflows.

quotes.quote: AI in medicine should be evaluated like a drug — for safety, for efficacy, and for the population it actually helps.
quotes.speaker: Eric Topol
quotes.title: Cardiologist; Director, Scripps Research Translational Institute

quotes.quote: We should stop training radiologists now. It is just completely obvious that within five years, deep learning is going to do better than radiologists.
quotes.speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
quotes.title: Turing Award laureate; ex-Google
quotes.source: Machine Learning and the Market for Intelligence conference, Toronto

quotes.quote: AI will not replace doctors, but doctors who use AI will replace doctors who don't.
quotes.speaker: Curtis Langlotz
quotes.title: Professor of Radiology and Biomedical Informatics, Stanford
